cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to find the best title for this passage. The question gives four options: A, B, C, D. The passage starts by talking about Will Steger taking people to the Arctic to show the effects of global warming. Steger is a polar explorer and environmentalist who's leading a team on a dogsled trip. They're going to upload content to a website so people can follow along. The main point seems to be about showing the effects of global warming through this expedition. Now, looking at the options. A) The Arctic Is In Danger: This mentions the Arctic being in danger, which is part of the story since the article talks about climate change affecting the Arctic. But is that the main focus? The expedition is a way to show the effects, so maybe this is a possible answer. B) Discoveries of the Arctic: Hmm. The article doesn't really talk about new discoveries. Instead, it's about documenting the effects of global warming. So maybe not B. C) Sledding Through the Arctic: The expedition is a dogsled trip, so this is literal. But is that the main point? The purpose is to show global warming effects, not just the act of sledding. So maybe C is too narrow. D) Consequences of Global Warming: This is a broader option. The passage does focus on the effects of global warming that the team will observe. But the title might need to connect the Arctic expedition to the consequences. The first paragraph says the author is going to take us north to see the effects, and the expedition is meant to show the front lines of global warming. So D could be a good fit. But the article also mentions that Steger is leading a team to document this. The title should encapsulate both the expedition and the message. A is about the Arctic in danger. The passage does say that the geography is changing and that reaching the North Pole by dogsled will be impossible soon. So A is also a contender. D is more general about consequences. Which one is better? The title should capture the main idea. The main idea is that Steger is taking a trip to show the consequences of global warming. The title needs to reflect both the expedition and the purpose. However, the options are separate. Looking at the options again. The passage's main focus is on the effects of global warming, using the Arctic expedition as an example. So D might be the best. But A is specific to the Arctic. The first sentence says "effects of global warming? Then head north," so the Arctic is a key example. The title could be either. But the question is about the best title.
